category.name: DSA
category.description: A Java-first DSA series covering complexity, patterns, data structures, dry runs, and interview problem-solving without hand-wavy explanations.
category.seoDescription: Learn data structures and algorithms in Java with practical guides on Big-O, two pointers, binary search, sliding window, trees, graphs, and interview patterns.

posts.title: B-Trees and B+ Trees in Java: The Engines of Modern Databases
posts.description: Understand the architecture of B-Trees and B+ Trees. Learn why these self-balancing trees are the standard for file systems and database indexing, and how they differ from Binary Search Trees.
posts.excerpt: While Binary Search Trees (BST) and AVL trees are great for in-memory operations, they perform poorly when data is stored on disk. This is where B-Trees and B+ Trees shine. They are the underlying data structures for alm…

posts.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
posts.description: Master Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java for finding shortest paths in weighted graphs, including those with negative edge weights. Learn its intuition, implementation, dry runs, and how to detect negative cycles.
posts.excerpt: Introduction to Bellman-Ford Algorithm Bellman-Ford Algorithm is a single-source shortest path algorithm that works on weighted graphs, including those with negative edge weights. Unlike Dijkstra's algorithm, which fails…

posts.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm in Java: Shortest Paths with Negative Weights
posts.description: Master the Bellman-Ford algorithm in Java. Learn how it handles negative edge weights and detects negative cycles in a weighted graph, and how it compares to Dijkstra.
posts.excerpt: While Dijkstra's algorithm is faster for most shortest-path problems, it fails when the graph contains negative edge weights. For those scenarios, we use the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Not only does it handle negative weigh…

posts.title: Bloom Filters in Java: Probabilistic Data Structures
posts.description: Master Bloom Filters in Java. Learn how this memory-efficient probabilistic data structure checks for set membership with zero false negatives and controllable false positives.
posts.excerpt: A Bloom Filter is a space-efficient probabilistic data structure used to test whether an element is a member of a set. It is unique because it can give two types of answers: 1. "Possibly in set": The element might be the…

posts.title: Kruskal’s Algorithm in Java: Minimum Spanning Trees (MST)
posts.description: Master Kruskal's Algorithm in Java for finding the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a connected, undirected, weighted graph. Learn its intuition, implementation with Union-Find, dry runs, and complexity analysis.
posts.excerpt: Introduction to Kruskal’s Algorithm Kruskal's Algorithm is another greedy algorithm used to find a Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) for a connected, undirected, weighted graph. Similar to Prim's, an MST connects all vertices …

posts.title: Matrix Traversal in Java: Spiral, Diagonal, and Flood Fill
posts.description: Master various matrix traversal techniques in Java, including spiral order, diagonal traversal, and flood fill. Learn their intuition, implementation, dry runs, and complexity analysis for solving common grid-based problems.
posts.excerpt: Introduction to Matrix Traversal Matrices (or 2D arrays) are fundamental data structures used to represent grids, images, game boards, and various other tabular data. Matrix traversal refers to the process of visiting ea…

posts.title: Prim’s Algorithm in Java: Minimum Spanning Trees (MST)

posts.excerpt: Introduction to Prim’s Algorithm Prim's Algorithm is a greedy algorithm used to find a Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) for a connected, undirected, weighted graph. An MST is a subgraph that connects all the vertices together…

posts.excerpt: Introduction to Breadth First Search (BFS) Breadth First Search (BFS) is a fundamental algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures. Unlike DFS, which explores as deeply as possible along each bran…

posts.description: A practical Java-first guide to Big-O notation for interviews. Learn how to analyze loops, recursion, collections, amortized complexity, and common mistakes with clear code examples.
